steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "baskets, which typically are woven from willow and ______ colorful beading." The blank is the second verb in a compound predicate joined by "and." Both verbs share the same subject ("which," referring to the baskets) and must use the same tense and form to maintain parallelism with the first verb, "are woven" (simple present).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘featured’: Simple past. "Are woven and featured" mixes present and past tense, breaking parallelism. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘had featured’: Past perfect. "Are woven and had featured" mixes present tense with past perfect, breaking parallelism. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘were featuring’: Past progressive. "Are woven and were featuring" mixes present and past, breaking parallelism. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘feature’: Simple present. "Are woven and feature" keeps both verbs in the present tense, maintaining parallelism throughout the relative clause. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
